skill.name: inngest
skill.description: Inngest expert for serverless-first background jobs, event-driven workflows, and durable execution without managing queues or workers.
skill.summary: Build reliable serverless background jobs and event-driven workflows with Inngest durable execution.
skill.author: sickn33

content.user_title: Build serverless background jobs with Inngest
content.value_statement: Creating reliable background processing without managing infrastructure is challenging. This skill helps you build durable, event-driven workflows using Inngest serverless functions.
actual_capabilities: Design and implement Inngest serverless functions with typed events
Build multi-step workflows with parallel processing and error handling
Create scheduled and cron-based functions for periodic tasks
Implement fan-out patterns for processing large event volumes
Configure concurrency controls to manage resource usage
Use durable sleep for long-running workflows spanning hours or days
limitations: Requires an Inngest account and cloud service or self-hosted deployment
Not suitable for real-time synchronous operations requiring immediate responses
Step functions must be idempotent to handle retries correctly
Event payloads should remain small to avoid performance issues

use_cases.title: AI Processing Pipeline
use_cases.description: Build multi-step AI workflows that process documents, generate summaries, and send notifications with durable execution across failures.

use_cases.title: User Onboarding System
use_cases.description: Create day-spanning onboarding flows with scheduled follow-ups, email sequences, and progress tracking without managing cron jobs.

use_cases.title: Event-Driven Data Sync
use_cases.description: Process high-volume events with fan-out patterns to sync data across services with built-in retry and concurrency controls.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Inngest Function
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic Inngest function that listens for user.created events and sends a welcome email. Include proper TypeScript types for the event payload.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Inngest event handling

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an Inngest workflow with three sequential steps: validate payment, provision resources, and send confirmation. Add error handling that retries failed steps.

prompt_templates.title: Scheduled Background Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Inngest function that runs daily at midnight to clean up expired sessions. Show how to configure the cron schedule and handle batch processing.

prompt_templates.title: Fan-Out Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Inngest workflow that processes 1000+ events in parallel using fan-out pattern. Include concurrency limits and progress tracking.
prompt_templates.scenario: Scaling to handle high-volume event processing

output_examples.input: Create a function to process new order events
output: Inngest function with typed event schema

output_examples.input: Build a workflow with parallel API calls
output: Fan-out step pattern for concurrent execution
Promise.all with step.run for parallel operations

best_practices: Always wrap external calls in step.run() for checkpointing and automatic retries
Keep event payloads small by passing IDs instead of full data objects
Configure appropriate concurrency limits to prevent resource exhaustion
anti_patterns: Avoid placing business logic outside of steps - it will not be checkpointed
Do not include large data blobs in events - use references instead
Never ignore step failures - implement proper error handling and retry logic

faq.question: What is Inngest and why use it?
faq.answer: Inngest is a serverless platform for building durable workflows. Unlike traditional background jobs, Inngest steps are checkpointed, meaning they survive failures and serverless cold starts.

faq.question: How does Inngest differ from AWS Step Functions?
faq.answer: Inngest works with any serverless platform (Vercel, Netlify, etc.) without vendor lock-in. It uses your existing code structure and provides a simpler developer experience with native TypeScript support.

faq.question: Can Inngest functions run for hours or days?
faq.answer: Yes. Inngest uses durable sleep that checkpoints your workflow state. Your function can pause for extended periods without consuming compute resources.

faq.question: How do I test Inngest functions locally?
faq.answer: Use the Inngest Dev Server for local development. It provides a UI to trigger events, inspect function runs, and debug workflows before deploying.

faq.question: What happens if a step fails?
faq.answer: Inngest automatically retries failed steps with exponential backoff. You can configure retry limits and add custom error handling logic.

faq.question: Do I need an Inngest cloud account?
faq.answer: Inngest offers both cloud and self-hosted options. The cloud service provides managed infrastructure, while self-hosting gives you full control over your deployment.
